label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. The question is about ordering the places from east to west. The options are given, and I need to figure out the correct one. The passage describes a hypothetical road from San Francisco to New York, with businesses lined up in order of size. First, the text says the line of flagpoles starts in San Francisco and goes to New York. So San Francisco is the westernmost, and New York is the easternmost. The first part of the journey, up to Reno, is where the businesses are smaller. Then from Reno eastward, the businesses get larger until Columbus, Ohio. Columbus is mentioned as being about four-fifths of the way across the nation. Then as you approach New York (the eastern terminus), the businesses get even larger. So the order from west to east is San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, New York. Therefore, from east to west, it would be the reverse: New York, Columbus, Reno, San Francisco. But the options given are about east to west order. Wait, the question says "from east to west". So the correct order would be New York, Columbus, Reno, San Francisco. Let's check the options. Looking at the options: (A) San Francisco, Reno, New York, Columbus – that's west to east starting, but the question is east to west. So this is from west to east. Not the right order. (B) San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, New York – same as A but with Columbus before New York. Still west to east. Not correct. (C) New York, Columbus, Reno, San Francisco – this is the reverse order. Since the road is from SF to New York, the order from east to west would be New York first, then Columbus (as it's four-fifths from SF, meaning closer to NY), then Reno, then SF. So this seems correct. (D) New York, Reno, Columbus, San Francisco – this would place Columbus east of Reno. But in the passage, Reno is west of Columbus. So Columbus is east of Reno. So D has Reno before Columbus, which is not correct. Because from New York going west, you pass Columbus first, then Reno. So in east to west order, it should be New York, Columbus, Reno, San Francisco. Which is option C. Wait, the passage says that from Reno eastward the poles increase in height until
